<div style="text-align:center"><div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto">
 <p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold;text-align:center" id="tx349509_1">PART I </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold;text-align:center">INTRODUCTION </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Unless otherwise indicated, all references herein to &#8220;Ericsson,&#8221; the &#8220;Company,&#8221; &#8220;the Group,&#8221; &#8220;we,&#8221; &#8220;us,&#8221; &#8220;our&#8221; or &#8220;our company&#8221; are references to Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson and its consolidated subsidiaries. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">This document is our Annual Report on Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F</span> for the year ended December&#160;31, 2022 (the &#8220;2022 Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F&#8221;).</span> Reference is made to the English version of our Swedish Annual Report for 2022, with certain adjustments made to comply with U.S. requirements which is attached hereto as Exhibit 15.1 (the &#8220;2022 Swedish Annual Report (adjusted version)&#8221;). Only (i)&#160;the information included in this 2022 Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F,</span> (ii)&#160;the information in the 2022 Swedish Annual Report (adjusted version) that is incorporated by reference in this 2022 Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F,</span> and (iii)&#160;the exhibits to the 2022 Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F</span> that are required to be filed pursuant to the Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F</span> shall be deemed to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission for any purpose, including incorporation by reference into the Registration Statement on Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">F-3</span> filed on March&#160;26, 2021 (File <span style="white-space:nowrap">No.333-254736)</span> and any other document filed by us pursuant to the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, which incorporates by reference the 2022 Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F.</span> Any information in the 2022 Swedish Annual Report (adjusted version) that is not referenced in the 2022 Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F</span> or filed as an exhibit thereto shall not be deemed to be so incorporated by reference. Certain industry, technical and financial terms used in this 2022 Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F</span> are defined in the subsections entitled &#8220;Glossary&#8221; and &#8220;Financial Terminology&#8221; of the 2022 Swedish Annual Report (adjusted version), which are incorporated herein by reference. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Market data and certain industry forecasts used herein were obtained from internal surveys, market research, publicly available information and industry publications. While we believe that the market research, publicly available information and industry publications we use are reliable, we have not independently verified market and industry data from third-party sources. Moreover, while we believe our internal surveys are reliable, they have not been verified by any independent source. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The information included on <span style="text-decoration:underline">http://www.ericsson.com/</span> and other websites that appear in this 2022 Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F</span> is not incorporated by reference herein. From time to time, we may use our website as a channel of distribution of material Company information. Financial and other material information regarding our company is routinely posted on and accessible at <span style="text-decoration:underline">http://www.ericsson.com/</span>. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Forward-Looking Statements </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">This 2022 Form <span style="white-space:nowrap">20-F</span> includes forward-looking statements, including statements reflecting the Company&#8217;s current views relating to the growth of the market, future market conditions, future events, financial condition, and expected operational and financial performance, including, in particular the following: </p> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>

INFORMATION ON THE COMPANY </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold" id="tx349509_10">A. History and Development of the Company </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold"><span style="font-style:italic">General facts on the company </span></p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Legal and commercial name of the Parent Company: </span>Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson (publ). </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Organization number: </span>556016-0680 </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Legal form of the Parent Company: </span>A Swedish limited liability company, organized under the Swedish Companies Act. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Country of incorporation: </span>Sweden. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Date of incorporation: </span>The Parent Company was incorporated on August&#160;18, 1918, as a result of a merger between AB LM Ericsson&#160;&amp; Co. and Stockholms Allm&#228;nna Telefon AB. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Domicile: </span>Our registered office is Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson, SE&#8211;164 83 Stockholm, Sweden. Our headquarters are located at Torshamnsgatan 21, Kista, Sweden. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Telephone number: </span>+46 10 719 0000 </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Website: </span><span style="text-decoration:underline">www.ericsson.com</span>. The information included on our website is not incorporated herein by reference. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In addition, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the &#8220;SEC&#8221;) maintains a website that contains reports, proxy and information statements, and other information regarding issuers that file electronically with the SEC: <span style="text-decoration:underline">http://www.sec.gov</span>. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Agent in the US: </span>Ericsson Inc., 6300 Legacy Drive, Plano, Texas 75024. Telephone number: +1 972 583 0000. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Shares: </span>Ericsson&#8217;s Class&#160;A and Class&#160;B shares are traded on Nasdaq Stockholm. In the US, our American Depository Shares (ADS), each representing one underlying Class&#160;B share, are traded on NASDAQ New York. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Parent Company operations: </span>The business of the Parent Company, Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson, consists mainly of corporate management, holding company functions and internal banking activities. Our Parent Company operations also include customer credit management activities performed by Ericsson Credit AB on a commission basis. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Subsidiaries and associated companies: </span>For a list of our significant subsidiaries, please see Item 4.C. &#8220;Shares owned directly by the Parent Company.&#8221; We are engaged in a number of minor joint ventures and cooperative arrangements. For more information regarding risks associated with joint ventures, strategic alliances and third-party agreements, please see &#8220;Item 3.D. Risk Factors.&#8221; </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold"><span style="font-style:italic">Company history and development </span></p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="text-decoration:underline">Innovating to empower people, business and society </span></p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Our origins date back to 1876 when Alexander Graham Bell filed a patent application in the United States for the telephone. The same year, Lars Magnus Ericsson opened a small workshop in Stockholm to repair telegraph instruments and sell his own telephone equipment. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Today, Ericsson enables communications service providers and enterprises to capture the full value of connectivity. The Company&#8217;s portfolio spans the following segments: Networks, Cloud Software and Services and Enterprise (which includes Enterprise Wireless Solutions, Global Communications Platform, and Technologies and New Businesses). In addition, segment Other includes media businesses as well as other <span style="white-space:nowrap">non-allocated</span> business. Our portfolio is designed to help our customers go digital, increase efficiency and find new revenue streams. Ericsson&#8217;s innovation investments have delivered the benefits of mobility and mobile broadband to billions of people globally. </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The information set forth under the following headings of the 2022 Swedish Annual Report (adjusted version) is incorporated herein by reference: </p> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>

<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:14%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:3%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:2%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Environmental, social and business conduct risk </p></td></tr></table> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:10%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:2%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Corporate Governance report </p></td></tr></table> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:14%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:3%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:2%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Regulation and compliance </p></td></tr></table> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Disclosure pursuant to Section</span><span style="font-weight:bold"></span><span style="font-weight:bold">&#160;219 of the Iran Threat Reduction and Syria Human Rights Act of 2012 (ITRA)</span> </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">During 2022, Ericsson recorded revenues relating to the sale of communications infrastructure related products and services in Iran to the following telecommunications company operating in the country: MTN Irancell. There are some transactions related to Mobile Communication Company of Iran (MCCI), primarily from internal clearing/accounting of pending old and minor items called as GR/IR (good receipt) clean up. During 2022, Ericsson&#8217;s gross revenue (reported as net sales) related to sales to MTN Irancell was SEK 106&#160;million. Ericsson does not normally allocate net profit (reported as net income) on a <span style="white-space:nowrap"><span style="white-space:nowrap">country-by-country</span></span> or <span style="white-space:nowrap"><span style="white-space:nowrap">activity-by-activity</span></span> basis, other than as set forth in Ericsson&#8217;s consolidated financial statements prepared in accordance with IFRS as issued by the IASB. However, Ericsson has estimated that its operating income (income before taxes and financial net) from such sales was, after internal cost allocation, SEK 29&#160;million during 2022. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In some instances, Ericsson has previously had to arrange performance bonds or similar financial guarantees to secure Ericsson&#8217;s performance of obligations under the commercial agreements Ericsson had entered into relating to the business in Iran. In such instances, Ericsson usually engaged its banks outside Iran, who in turn engaged local banks in the country. These local banks include Tejarat Bank, Melli Bank, Parsian Bank and Saderat Bank. Although some bonds and guarantees are still in place, no new performance bonds or similar guarantees involving these banks with respect to Ericsson&#8217;s business activities in Iran were issued during 2022. During 2022, existing bank guarantees issued by Maskan Bank, Parsian Bank, Post Bank of Iran, Bank Mellat and Tejarat Bank (local banks in Iran) to secure Iranian customer payment obligations to Ericsson were renewed. Further, some payments made to Ericsson&#8217;s local subsidiary and payments required to be made by the local subsidiary to suppliers involve banks that may be controlled by the government of Iran. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Since 2018, Ericsson has reduced its business engagement as well as its presence in Iran significantly but has continued to provide certain critical services and support to telecommunications networks and systems. However, as the complexity associated with operating in the country continues to increase, Ericsson is planning for an orderly wind down as soon as practicable. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold" id="tx349509_12"><span id="tx349509_222"></span>C. Organizational Structure </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The following list shows certain shareholdings owned directly and indirectly by our Parent Company as of December&#160;31, 2022. A complete list of shareholdings, prepared in accordance with the Swedish Annual Accounts Act and filed with the Swedish Companies Registration Office (Bolagsverket), may be obtained upon request to: Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson, External Reporting, <span style="white-space:nowrap">SE-164</span> 83 Stockholm, Sweden. </p>
 <p style="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&#160;</p> <p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;text-align:center">3 </p>

</div></div>

Property, Plants and Equipment </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold"><span style="font-style:italic">Primary manufacturing and assembly facilities </span></p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">We continuously adjust our production capacity to meet expected customer demand. During 2022, our overall capacity utilization* was 53%. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The table below summarizes where we have major sites and the total floor space at <span style="white-space:nowrap">year-end.</span> All facilities are leased, other than Nanjing (China). The majority of the floor space within our production facilities is used for assembly and test. </p> <p style="font-size:12pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>

<td style="width:10%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:2%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Regulation and compliance </p></td></tr></table> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">As announced in May 2022, we began to report financial information according to our new segment structure as of the third quarter 2022. Accordingly, below we provide a discussion of our operating results for 2021 compared 2020 using financial information that has been restated to reflect </p> <p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">the new segment structure. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">2021 highlights </p> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:5%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:3%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Reported sales were stable at SEK 232.3&#160;billion. Group sales growth adjusted for comparable units and currency was 4%, with an increase in Networks of 7%. The loss of market share in Mainland China impacted sales by SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-7.7&#160;billion.</span> and the growth rate by <span style="white-space:nowrap">-3</span> percentage points, meaning that excluding Mainland China, sales growth adjusted for comparable units and currency was 8%. </p></td></tr></table> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:5%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:3%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Reported net income improved to SEK 23.0 (17.6) billion. Earnings per share (EPS) diluted was SEK 6.81 (5.26). </p></td></tr></table> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">

<td style="width:5%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:3%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Gross income was SEK 100.7 (93.7) billion, driven by strengthened operation leverage in Networks. Reported gross margin was 43.4% (40.3%). </p></td></tr></table> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:5%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:3%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Reported EBIT improved to SEK 31.8 (27.8) billion while the EBIT margin improved to 13.7% (12.0%). </p></td></tr></table> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:5%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:3%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">EBIT margin excluding restructuring charges improved to 13.9% (12.5%), reaching the 2022 group target already in 2021. </p></td></tr></table> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:5%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:3%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Cash flow from operating activities was SEK 39.1 (28.9) billion. Free cash flow before M&amp;A amounted to SEK 32.1 (22.3) billion. Cash and cash equivalents were SEK 54.1 (43.6) billion on December&#160;31, 2021. Net cash was SEK 65.8 (41.9) billion on December&#160;31, 2021. </p></td></tr></table> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:5%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:3%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">The Board of Directors proposed a dividend for 2021 of SEK 2.50 (2.00) per share to the AGM. </p></td></tr></table> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Business in 2021 </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In 2021, reported sales were stable at SEK 232.3&#160;billion. A stronger Swedish krona (SEK) had a negative impact on reported sales in all segments. Sales growth adjusted for comparable units and currency was 4%, mainly driven by growth in Networks. Networks reported sales grew by 1%, while sales growth adjusted for comparable units and currency was 7%, primarily supported by increased product sales as a result of continued market share gains. From a geographical point of view, sales growth was primarily underpinned by North America, Europe and Latin America as well as in some North East Asia markets. The sales in Mainland China declined by SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-7.7&#160;billion,</span> due to reduced market share, impacting Group organic growth rate by <span style="white-space:nowrap">-3</span> percentage points. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported sales decreased in Cloud Software and Services by <span style="white-space:nowrap">-6%,</span> mainly due to reduced variable sales in network managed services in a large contract in North America, after the merger between two operators. Contract rescoping and planned exits also contributed to the sales decline. IPR licensing revenues decreased to SEK 8.1 (10.0) billion, mainly due to lower volumes with one licensee. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported gross margin improved to 43.4% (40.3%), primarily driven by strengthened operational leverage in Networks. A higher share of product revenues in the sales mix had a positive impact on the gross margin. Cloud Software and Services gross margin improved to 33.5% (32.7%) mainly as an effect of efficiency gains. Gross margin in Enterprise increased, driven by the acquired Cradlepoint business, which has a higher average gross margin than the other businesses in the Enterprise segment. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Operating expenses increased to SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-69.1</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-66.3)</span> billion. Research and development (R&amp;D) expenses increased in Networks, Cloud Software and Services and Enterprise. Selling and administrative (SG&amp;A) expenses increased to SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-27.0</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-26.7)</span> billion through investments in the acquired Cradlepoint business. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Restructuring charges decreased to SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-0.5</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-1.3)</span> billion. The restructuring charges in 2021 were mainly related to Mainland China. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">EBIT was SEK 31.8 (27.8) billion. The improvement was driven by improved gross income in segment Networks. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The number of employees increased to 101,322 (100,824). The increase was mainly due to Research and Development, which increased by 1,210 employees. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Cash flow from operating activities was SEK 39.1 (28.9) billion. Free cash flow before M&amp;A amounted to SEK 32.1 (22.3) billion. The improvement was driven by improved profitability. Cash and cash equivalents were SEK 54.1 (43.6) billion on December&#160;31, 2022. Net cash at December&#160;31, 2021 was SEK 65.8 (41.9) billion. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Financial highlights </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Net sales </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported sales were stable at SEK 232.3 (232.4) billion. Sales in Mainland China declined by SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-7.7&#160;billion,</span> impacting Group sales growth adjusted for comparable units and currency by <span style="white-space:nowrap">-3</span> percentage points. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Networks sales increased by SEK 1.9&#160;billion, or 1%, to SEK 167.8&#160;billion, with a negative impact of SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-6.4&#160;billion</span> from reduced market share in Mainland China. Cloud Software and Services sales decreased by SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-3.4&#160;billion,</span> or <span style="white-space:nowrap">-6%,</span> to SEK 56.2&#160;billion, with an impact of SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-1.3&#160;billion</span> due to the reduced market share in Mainland China. Enterprise sales increased by SEK 1.4&#160;billion, or 30%, to SEK 6.2&#160;billion, driven mainly by Cradlepoint which has a higher average gross margin than the other businesses in the Enterprise segment. Group sales growth adjusted for comparable units and currency increased by 4%, while excluding Mainland China, organic sales growth was 8%. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">IPR licensing revenues declined to SEK 8.1 (10.0) billion, mainly due to lower volumes with one licensee. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Networks sales growth adjusted for comparable units and currency increased by 7%. Sales growth was primarily driven by North America, Europe and Latin America. Networks accounted for 72% (71%) of Group sales. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported sales in Cloud Software and Services sales declined by <span style="white-space:nowrap">-6%</span> mainly due to reduced variable sales in network managed services in a large contract in North America, after the merger between two operators. Contract rescoping and planned exits also contributed to the sales decline. Cloud Software and Services accounted for 24% (26%) of Group sales. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported sales in Enterprise grew by 30%, primarily driven by Cradlepoint. Enterprise accounted for 3% (2%) of Group sales. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In the market area dimension, sales growth was driven by North America, and Europe and Latin America. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The sales mix by commodity was: hardware 46% (41%), software 20% (22%) and services 34% (37%). </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Gross margin </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported gross margin increased to 43.4% (40.3%). Gross margin excluding restructuring charges improved to 43.5% (40.6%) driven primarily by strengthened operational leverage in Networks. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Research and development (R&amp;D) expenses </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported R&amp;D expenses increased to SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-42.1</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-39.7)</span> billion. R&amp;D expenses increased in Networks and Cloud Software and Services due to increased investments in the segments&#8217; 5G portfolios and in Enterprise as a result of the acquired Cradlepoint business. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Selling and administrative (SG&amp;A) expenses </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">SG&amp;A expenses increased to SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-27.0</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-26.7)</span> billion. Selling expenses increased through investments in expanding the sales force in the acquired Cradlepoint business. Revaluation of customer financing was SEK 0.4 <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-0.3)</span> billion.<span style="font-weight:bold"> </span> </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Impairment losses on trade receivables </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reversal of impairment losses on trade receivables was SEK 0.0 (0.1) billion. </p>
 <p style="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&#160;</p> <p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;text-align:center">6 </p>

</div></div>

<h5><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<div style="text-align:center"><div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto">
 <p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Other operating income and expenses </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Other operating income and expenses was SEK 0.4 (0.7) billion. Share in earnings of JVs and associated companies was SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-0.3</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-0.3)</span> billion. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Restructuring charges </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Restructuring charges decreased to SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-0.5</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-1.3)</span> billion. Restructuring charges in 2021 were mainly related to Mainland China. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Earning before financial items and income taxes (EBIT) </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported EBIT improved to SEK 31.8 (27.8) billion with an EBIT margin of 13.7% (12.0%). EBIT excluding restructuring charges improved to SEK 32.3 (29.1) billion with an EBIT margin excluding restructuring charges of 13.9% (12.5%). The improvement was driven by improved gross income in segment Networks. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Net income </span> </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Net income improved to SEK 23.0 (17.6) billion, driven by improved EBIT and lower reported taxes. Earnings per share (EPS) diluted was SEK 6.81 (5.26) and Adjusted EPS was SEK 7.26 (5.83). </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">EBITA </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">EBITA improved to SEK 33.3 (29.0) billion with an EBITA margin of 14.3% (12.5%). EBITA excluding restructuring charges increased to SEK 33.8 (30.3) billion corresponding to an EBITA margin excluding restructuring charges of 14.6% (13.1%). </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Financial income and expenses, net </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Financial income and expenses, net declined to SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-2.5</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-0.6)</span> billion, mainly due to impact from the currency hedge. The currency hedge effect impacted financial income and expenses, net by SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-0.8</span> (1.0) billion. The USD strengthened against the SEK between December&#160;31, 2020 (SEK/USD rate 8.19) and December&#160;31, 2021 (SEK/USD rate 9.05). </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Taxes </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Taxes were SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-6.3</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-9.6)</span> billion, positively impacted by utilization of impaired withholding tax assets in Sweden. The effective tax rate in 2021 was 21%, while the effective 2020 tax rate was approximately 35%. Effective tax rate excluding utilization of impaired withholding tax assets in Sweden would have been 25%. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Employees </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The number of employees on December&#160;31, 2021 was 101,322, an increase of 498 employees compared with December&#160;31, 2020. In Research and Development, the number of employees increased by 1,210. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Cash flow </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Cash flow from operating activities </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported cash flow from operating activities was SEK 39.1 (28.9) billion. The improvement was attributed to both improved EBIT and decreased operating net assets. Cash flow from operating activities in 2020 was impacted by payments of SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-3.0&#160;billion</span> into the Swedish Pension Trust. Operating net assets decreased for the full year with a positive impact on cash flow from operating activities of SEK 4.0&#160;billion. Key movements include a negative impact of SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-5.6&#160;billion</span> related to an increase in inventory, mainly driven by the decision to strengthen the supply chain resilience within Networks. The negative impact was partly offset by a positive impact of SEK 1.4&#160;billion from an increase in trade payables. Cash flow from operating activities was also positively impacted by SEK 4.0&#160;billion from an increase in contract liabilities. Provisions of SEK 4.2 (4.0) billion were utilized, of which SEK 0.8 (0.8) billion related to restructuring charges. Taxes paid were SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-4.1&#160;billion.</span> </p> <p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Accounts receivable days of sales outstanding increased to 71 (69) days and working capital days were stable at 65 (65) days. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Free cash flow </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Free cash flow before M&amp;A was SEK 32.1 (22.3) billion, or 13.8% (9.6%), in relation to net sales, compared with the long-term target of <span style="white-space:nowrap">9-12%.</span> Capex net and other investing activities were SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-4.6</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-4.3)</span> billion. Repayment of lease liabilities was SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-2.4</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-2.4)</span> billion. There were few M&amp;A transactions settled in 2021, and free cash flow after M&amp;A was SEK 32.1 (12.7) billion. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Cash flow from investing activities </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported cash flow from investing activities was SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-19.9</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-15.2)</span> billion as a result of purchases of interest-bearing securities. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Cash flow from financing activities </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported cash flow from financing activities was SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-9.3</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-12.5)</span> billion including repayment of lease liabilities. During the year, dividends of SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-6.9</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-6.0)</span> billion were paid to shareholders and the net impact on cash flow from issuance and repayment of long-term debt was SEK 2.1&#160;billion. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Financial position </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Gross cash was SEK 97.6 (72.0) billion as a result of the positive free cash flow from operating activities and a SEK 2.6&#160;billion loan with the European Investment Bank (EIB), partly offset by SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-6.9</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-6.0)</span> billion of dividends paid to shareholders. Net cash was SEK 65.8 (41.9) billion. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Liabilities for post-employment benefits decreased to SEK 36.1 (37.4) billion, primarily due to positive asset returns. The Swedish defined benefit obligation (DBO) was calculated using a discount rate based on the yields of Swedish government bonds. If the discount rate had been based on Swedish covered mortgage bonds, the liability for post-employment benefits would have been approximately SEK 17.3&#160;billion (SEK 18.8&#160;billion lower than current DBO). </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The average maturity of long-term borrowings was 3.5 years as of December&#160;31, 2021, an increase from 2.7 years 12 months earlier. In March 2021, Ericsson repaid its EUR <span style="white-space:nowrap">-0.5&#160;billion</span> (SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-5.1</span> billion) bond, and in May 2021, Ericsson issued a EUR 0.5&#160;billion (SEK 5.0 billion) senior unsecured eight-year bond. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In September 2021, Ericsson renewed its existing USD 2.0&#160;billion revolving credit facility, linked to two of Ericsson&#8217;s long-term sustainability goals. The facility has a five-year tenure with two <span style="white-space:nowrap">one-year</span> extension options and is undrawn. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Standard&#160;&amp; Poor&#8217;s and Fitch have a long-term <span style="white-space:nowrap">BBB-</span> rating on Ericsson with stable outlook. Moody&#8217;s has a Ba1 rating with stable outlook. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The capital turnover decreased to 1.3 (1.4) times, while Return on capital employed (ROCE) improved to 18.4% (17.0%) driven by improved EBIT. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Research and development, patents and licensing </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In 2021, R&amp;D expenses amounted to SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-42.1</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-39.7)</span> billion. R&amp;D expenses were impacted by SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-0.1</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-0.4)</span> billion of restructuring charges. The number of R&amp;D resources increased to 27,379 (26,169) and the number of patents continued to increase and amounted to more than 60,000 (57,000) granted patents by end of 2021. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Seasonality </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The Company&#8217;s sales, income and cash flow from operating activities vary between quarters and are generally lowest in the first quarter of the year and highest in the fourth quarter. This is mainly a result of the seasonal purchase patterns of telecom operators. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold"><span style="white-space:nowrap">Off-balance</span> sheet arrangements </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">There were no <span style="white-space:nowrap">off-balance</span> sheet arrangements that have, or would be reasonably likely to have, a current or anticipated material effect on the Company&#8217;s financial condition, revenues, expenses, result of operations, liquidity, capital expenditures or capital resources. </p>
 <p style="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&#160;</p> <p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;text-align:center">7 </p>

</div></div>



<p style="margin-top:1em; margin-bottom:0em; page-break-before:always"> </p>
<hr style="color:#999999;height:3px;width:100%" />
<h5><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


<div style="text-align:center"><div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto">
 <p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Capital expenditures </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">For 2021, capital expenditures were SEK 3.7 (4.5) billion, representing 1.6% of sales. Expenditures are largely related to test sites and equipment for R&amp;D, network operation centers and manufacturing and repair operations. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Annual capital expenditures are normally around 2% of sales. This corresponds to the needs for keeping and maintaining the current capacity level. The Board of Directors reviews the Company&#8217;s investment plans and proposals. As of December&#160;31, 2021, no material land, buildings, machinery or equipment were pledged as collateral for outstanding indebtedness. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Capitalized development expenses </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Capitalized development expenses increased to SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-1.0</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-0.8)</span> billion due to 5G development projects. The net effect on operating income of capitalized and amortized development expenses was SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-0.1</span> (0.2) billion. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Business results &#8211; Segments </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Networks </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Networks represented 72% (71%) of Group net sales in 2021. Networks offers multi-technology capable Radio Access Network (RAN) solutions for all network spectrum bands, including integrated high-performing hardware and software. The offering also includes a cloud-native RAN portfolio, a transport portfolio, passive and active antenna solutions and a complete service portfolio covering network deployment and support. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Net sales </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported sales increased by 1% in 2021 to SEK 167.8 (166.0) billion. Growth was driven primarily by increased product sales as a result of continued market share gains. Sales growth adjusted for comparable units and currency increased by 7%. Sales growth was underpinned by increased sales in North America and in Europe and Latin America as well as in some North East Asian markets. Sales declined by SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-6.4&#160;billion</span> year-over-year in Mainland China, impacting the growth rate adjusted for comparable units and currency by <span style="white-space:nowrap">-4</span> percentage points. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Gross margin </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported gross margin increased to 47.0% (43.6%), as a result of continued strengthening of operational leverage and a higher share of product revenues in the sales mix. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">EBIT </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported EBIT increased to SEK 37.3 (30.9) billion with an increase in EBIT margin to 22.2% (18.6%). EBIT excluding restructuring charges improved to SEK 37.5 (31.6) billion with an EBIT margin excluding restructuring charges of 22.4% (19.0%) driven by sales growth and improved gross margin. R&amp;D investments in the 5G portfolio increased during the year, while selling and administrative expenses decreased. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Cloud Software and Services </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Cloud Software and Services represented 24% (26%) of Group net sales in 2021. Cloud Software and Services provides solutions for core networks, business and operational support systems, network design and optimization, and network managed services. The focus is to enable communications service providers to succeed in their transition to cloud native, intelligent and automated networks and operations. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Net sales </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported sales decreased by <span style="white-space:nowrap">-6%</span> to SEK 56.2 (59.6) billion in 2021, mainly due to reduced variable sales in network managed services in a large contract in North America, after the merger between two operators. Sales in Mainland China decreased by SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-1.3&#160;billion</span> year-over-year. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Gross margin </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported gross margin increased to 33.5% (32.7%), while gross margin excluding restructuring charges increased to 33.9% (33.2%), mainly as a result of efficiency gains, partially offset by the negative impact of initial 5G Core deployment costs. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">EBIT (loss) </span>Reported EBIT (loss) was SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-2.2</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-0.8)</span> billion. EBIT excluding restructuring charges was SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-2.0</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-0.5)</span> billion.<span style="font-weight:bold"> </span></p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Enterprise </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Segment Enterprise represented 3% (2%) of Group net sales in 2021. The segment comprises of three Business Areas offering solutions primarily to Enterprise: Global Communications Platform (Vonage) including cloud-based Unified Communications as a Service (UCaaS), Contact Center as a Service (CCaaS) and Communications Platform as a Service (CPaaS); Enterprise Wireless Solutions including private wireless networks and wireless WAN (Cradlepoint) <span style="white-space:nowrap">pre-packaged</span> solutions; Technologies and New Businesses including mobile financial services, security solutions and advertising services. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Net sales </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported sales increased by 30% to SEK 6.2 (4.8) billion, driven by the acquired Cradlepoint business. Cradlepoint saw increasing demand for the 5G portfolio during the year. Reported sales and margins for Cradlepoint were in line with the acquisition plan. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Gross margin </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported gross margin increased to 46.4% (36.9%), while gross margin excluding restructuring charges increased to 46.4% (39.8%) driven by Cradlepoint. Cradlepoint has a higher average gross margin that the other business in the Enterprise segment. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">EBIT (loss) </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported EBIT (loss) was SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-3.0</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-1.9),</span> while EBIT excluding restructuring charges was SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-2.9</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-1.7)</span> billion driven by the acquired Cradlepoint business. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Other </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Segment Other represented 1% (1%) of Group net sales in 2021. Segment Other includes media businesses as well as other <span style="white-space:nowrap">non-allocated</span> business. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Net sales </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported sales was stable at SEK 2.0&#160;billion. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Gross margin </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported gross margin was 7.9% (2.4%), while gross margin excluding restructuring charges was 8.3% (3.0%). </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">EBIT (loss) </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Reported EBIT (loss) was SEK <span style="white-space:nowrap">-0.3</span> <span style="white-space:nowrap">(-0.3)</span> billion. The 2021 result was impacted by the Nokia settlement related to the 2019 resolution with the U.S. authorities and impairment <span style="white-space:nowrap">write-off.</span> EBIT was positively impacted by SEK 1&#160;billion through a positive revaluation of Ericsson investments and data center divestment. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold" id="tx349509_17">B. Liquidity and Capital Resources </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The information set forth under the following headings of the 2022 Swedish Annual Report (adjusted version) is incorporated herein by reference: </p> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>

<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Note H3 &#8211; Statement of cash flows </p></td></tr></table> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; margin-left:4%; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">See Item 8.B. &#8220;Financial Information &#8211; Significant Changes&#8221; herein. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold" id="tx349509_18">C. Research and Development, Patents and Licenses, etc. </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The information set forth under the following headings of the 2022 Swedish Annual Report (adjusted version) is incorporated herein by reference: </p> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>

<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">

<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">

<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:5%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:3%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Board of Directors&#8217; report </p></td></tr></table> <p style="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>
<table style="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;border:0;width:100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0">
<tr style="page-break-inside:avoid">
<td style="width:10%">&#160;</td>
<td style="width:2%;vertical-align:top" align="left">&#8226;</td>
<td style="width:1%;vertical-align:top">&#160;</td>
<td align="left" style="vertical-align:top"> <p style=" margin-top:0pt ; margin-bottom:0pt; font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;text-align:left">Financial highlights &#8211; Research and development, patents and licensing </p></td></tr></table> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">Ericsson has a policy of strong commitment to research and development (R&amp;D) with substantial contributions to cutting-edge open standards and technologies, leading the development of cellular technology &#8211; from 2G to 5G and beyond. Our strong R&amp;D investments position us to further extend our technology leadership for cost performance and sustainability. It is Ericsson&#8217;s policy to protect and capitalize on our R&amp;D investments by creating, securing, protecting, and licensing a portfolio of patents in support of our overall business goals. Our patent portfolio comprises more than 60,000 granted patents, which are licensed globally on FRAND terms to the users of our technology. Ericsson is one of the largest contributors to <span style="white-space:nowrap">O-RAN,</span> driving the alignment between 3GPP and Open RAN and investing heavily in Cloud RAN to ensure long-term competitiveness. Within Enterprise, Ericsson is increasing investments in developing the network APIs. Other areas of focus in R&amp;D include the capability of networks to handle gigabytes per second more efficiently, using more automation, using Artificial Intelligence to improve network performance and reducing energy consumption, while securing sustainability leadership to support customers to lower the total cost of ownership and meet net zero requirements. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Research and Development and Patent Information </p> <p style="font-size:12pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&#160;</p>

<div style="text-align:center"><div style="width:8.5in;text-align:left;margin-left: auto;margin-right: auto">
 <p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In addition, the Nomination Committee informed the Company that the current chairman, Ronnie Leten, and board members Kurt Jofs and Nora Denzel had informed the Nomination Committee that they will not stand for <span style="white-space:nowrap">re-election</span> at the Annual General Meeting 2023. The Company expects to hold its Annual General Meeting on March&#160;29, 2023, and the Nomination Committee&#8217;s complete proposals and motivated statement are available on the Company&#8217;s website www.ericsson.com. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman"><span style="font-weight:bold">Update on Deferred Prosecution Agreement </span> </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">In 2019, Ericsson entered into a deferred prosecution agreement (DPA) with the United States Department of Justice (DOJ) in order to resolve past (prior to 2017) Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) violations relating to misconduct in certain countries. The DPA provided that, in the event of any breach of its ongoing DPA obligations, the Company could be prosecuted for the historical FCPA violations covered by the DPA. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">As announced in October 2021 and March 2022, the DOJ notified Ericsson that it failed to provide certain documents and information to the DOJ in a timely manner and did not adequately report to the DOJ certain information relating to the 2019 internal Iraq investigation. The DOJ has not alleged or charged Ericsson with any new criminal misconduct since the start of the DPA. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">The Company&#8217;s internal investigation and its cooperation with authorities in relation to the matters discussed in a 2019 internal Iraq investigation report remain open and ongoing. With respect to the matters described in the 2019 internal Iraq investigation report, the Company continues to thoroughly investigate the matters in full cooperation with the DOJ and the SEC. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">As previously disclosed, the Company&#8217;s 2019 investigation did not conclude that Ericsson made or was responsible for any payments to any terrorist organization and significant further investigation over the course of 2022 has not altered this conclusion. Since 2019, Ericsson has taken significant remedial measures, overseen by the Board of Directors. These include enhancing its group-wide approach to risk management and strengthening its compliance program and internal controls. The Company agreed in December 2022 to extend its independent compliance monitorship with one year, until June 2024, to further our efforts to embed <span style="white-space:nowrap"><span style="white-space:nowrap">best-in-class</span></span> compliance, risk management and internal controls across the organization. On March&#160;2, 2023, the Company reached a resolution (DOJ Plea Agreement) with the DOJ regarding <span style="white-space:nowrap">non-criminal</span> breaches under its DPA. Under the DOJ Plea Agreement, Ericsson will plead guilty to previously deferred charges relating to conduct prior to 2017. In addition, Ericsson has agreed to pay a fine of USD 206,728,848. The entry of the DOJ Plea Agreement will bring the DPA to an end. In the fourth quarter of 2022, the Company made a provision of SEK 2.3&#160;billion (approx. USD 220 million) in relation to the DOJ Plea Agreement, including estimated expenses (SEK 0.1 billion) for the extended compliance monitorship. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold">Ericsson announces changes to the Executive Team </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">On January&#160;25, 2023, Ericsson announced that Jenny Lindqvist has been appointed as Senior Vice President, Head of Market Area Europe&#160;&amp; Latin America, as of February&#160;1, 2023. Effective the same date she will become member of the Ericsson Executive Team, reporting to the CEO. Jenny Lindqvist has a Master of Science in Business&#160;&amp; Economics from Stockholm School of Economics. Previous management positions within Ericsson Business Area and Market Area organizations include Head of Global Customer Unit Telia Company, Head of Solution Line Intelligent Transport Systems, Key Account Manager Telenor, Managed Services Engagement Lead and Business Manager Multimedia. Previous positions outside Ericsson include roles in management consulting in France and Sweden, as well as in Pharmaceuticals in the Philippines. </p> <p style="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman">As a member of Ericsson&#8217;s Executive Leadership Team, Jenny Lindqvist succeeds Stefan Koetz who has been acting in this role as of June&#160;1, 2022. Stefan will take on a new role as Head of Strategic Projects for Market Area Europe&#160;&amp; Latin America. </p> <p style="margin-top:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold" id="tx349509_34">ITEM&#160;9. THE OFFER AND LISTING </p> <p style="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:Times New Roman;font-weight:bold" id="tx349509_35">A.
